title: Types with only static members should be static
messageFormat: This type exposes only static members and no instance state; declare it static.
summary: A type that carries no instance state and exposes only static members is never meant to be instantiated. Leaving it as a normal class invites callers to create useless instances and hides the type's real intent. Marking it static makes the compiler enforce that no instance is ever created and signals at a glance that the type is a pure utility holder.
